agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Direct string interpolation of user input into SQL query creates a textbook SQL injection vulnerability in a public-facing endpoint."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized user input","risk":"An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter query parameters, enabling data exfiltration, modification, or deletion of the entire database.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (f-string query construction)","reproduction":"Send a request with customer_id set to: ' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --  The resulting query becomes: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --' AND status = '...'  This bypasses the WHERE clause and can execute destructive statements.","fix":"Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter)). If using psycopg2, use %s placeholders. Never interpolate user input into SQL strings.","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security, data integrity, regulatory compliance (PCI/SOC2 if applicable)"},{"title":"No input validation on public-facing parameters","risk":"Even after fixing the injection, there is no validation that customer_id or status_filter are well-formed. A caller can query any customer's deliveries (IDOR) or pass garbage status values causing unexpected query behavior.","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function signature and body","reproduction":"Call search_deliveries(db, some_other_customers_id, 'anything') — no check that the authenticated user owns this customer_id, and no check that status_filter is a valid enum value.","fix":"1) Validate that the authenticated user is authorized to query the given customer_id (compare against session/token). 2) Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., 'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered'). Reject with 400/403 otherwise.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Authorization, API correctness"},{"title":"SELECT * returns unbounded results with no pagination","risk":"A broad query (valid customer with many deliveries) returns the entire result set in memory. Under load or for high-volume customers, this causes OOM or extreme latency on the public endpoint, enabling a denial-of-service.","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function, query and fetchall()","reproduction":"Query a customer_id with 500k+ delivery rows. fetchall() loads every row into memory at once, starving the application server.","fix":"Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination. Replace SELECT * with explicit column selection. Consider streaming results with fetchmany() or server-side cursors for large datasets.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Performance, availability, scalability"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"SELECT * is fragile and wasteful","description":"SELECT * couples the query to the full table schema. Adding a column (e.g., a large BLOB) silently increases payload size and can break serialization. Select only the columns the API contract requires.","location":"search_deliveries query"},{"title":"No error handling","description":"db_conn.execute can raise exceptions (connection lost, syntax error, timeout). The function has no try/except, so database errors will propagate as unhandled 500s with potentially sensitive stack traces in the public API response.","location":"search_deliveries function body"}],"suggestions":["Add logging/metrics on query execution time to detect slow queries before they impact users","Consider adding an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not already exist — this query will be called frequently from a public endpoint","Return a well-defined data class or dict rather than raw Row objects from fetchall() to decouple the API response from the DB schema"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code must not be deployed. The SQL injection vulnerability is exploitable from the public internet with zero authentication bypass required — the inputs come directly from query parameters.","rollback_capability":"If already deployed, rollback is urgent but insufficient — the database may already be compromised. Rotate credentials and audit query logs.","backward_compatibility":"Fixing the SQL injection (parameterized queries) is backward-compatible. Adding input validation and pagination may require API contract updates if callers depend on current behavior.","monitoring_coverage":"No monitoring exists in this code. At minimum, add query latency metrics and error rate tracking before deploying any version.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["auth_security: No authorization check means any authenticated (or unauthenticated) user can query any customer's deliveries"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix the SQL injection with parameterized queries, add authorization and input validation, then deploy behind a feature flag with query logging enabled."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":98,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is unambiguous and confirmed by code inspection","IDOR risk is clear from the lack of any authorization check","Unbounded fetchall() is visible in the code"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot confirm whether an ORM or middleware layer exists upstream that might partially mitigate some risks — reviewing only this function in isolation"],"assumptions":["This function is called directly from the HTTP handler with raw query parameters as stated","No upstream SQL sanitization or WAF is in place","db_conn.execute supports parameterized queries (standard for Python DB-API 2.0)"]}}
